channel.title: Last Day - NEW & UPDATE
channel.description: All NEW and UPDATE articles from the last day with enhanced metadata

item.title: NEW: Ransomware Landscape Fragments with 61 New Groups Emerging in 2026
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Landscape Fragments with 61 New Groups Emerging in 2026

Summary:
A Black Kite report reveals a significant fragmentation in the ransomware landscape, with 61 new groups emerging in 2026, bringing the total to 146. This proliferation has shrunk the average group's lifespan to just under five months. Despite the influx of new actors, a small number of established players, including Qilin and Akira, continue to dominate, accounting for 44% of all publicly disclosed victims between March 2025 and March 2026. This trend indicates a dynamic and crowded threat environment where new, ephemeral groups operate alongside highly active, persistent ones.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: A Black Kite report reveals the ransomware ecosystem is fragmenting, with 61 new groups in 2026. Qilin and Akira remain top threats amid this trend.

Article Slug: ransomware-ecosystem-fragments-as-61-new-groups-emerge-in-2026

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Vulnerabilities Surge 51% as AI-Driven Supply Chain Attacks Rise
item.description:
Headline: Vulnerabilities Surge 51% as AI-Driven Supply Chain Attacks Rise

Summary:
A Forescout report for H1 2026 reveals a 51% surge in published vulnerabilities and a 25% increase in claimed ransomware attacks. The study highlights the growing abuse of AI by threat actors to accelerate campaigns, the continued risk of sophisticated supply chain attacks, and the persistent targeting of IoT and OT devices. A notable example was the Vercel OAuth breach, where attackers used Lumma Stealer to compromise a third-party integration and harvest credentials, demonstrating the complex, multi-stage nature of modern threats.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Forescout's H1 2026 report shows a 51% increase in vulnerabilities and a 25% rise in ransomware, highlighting threats from AI and supply chain attacks.

Article Slug: forescout-report-highlights-51-percent-surge-in-vulnerabilities-and-ai-driven-threats

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Q2 Ransomware Attacks Up 3%; VPNs Remain Top Initial Access Vector
item.description:
Headline: Q2 Ransomware Attacks Up 3%; VPNs Remain Top Initial Access Vector

Summary:
According to NCC Group's Q2 2026 threat report, global ransomware attacks increased by 3% to 2,229 incidents. The industrial sector was most targeted, and North America remained the most impacted region. For the fifth straight quarter, the Qilin ransomware group was the most active. The report critically highlights that corporate VPNs and internet-facing edge devices are the primary initial access vector, with groups like Qilin and Akira actively exploiting vulnerabilities in products from Fortinet, Citrix, SonicWall, and Check Point.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: NCC Group's Q2 2026 report shows a 3% increase in ransomware attacks, with threat actors like Qilin exploiting VPN vulnerabilities as the primary initial access vector.

Article Slug: q2-2026-ransomware-attacks-rise-3-percent-with-vpns-as-top-entry-point

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: DoD Suspends CMMC Phase II, Initiates 60-Day Program Review
item.description:
Headline: DoD Suspends CMMC Phase II, Initiates 60-Day Program Review

Summary:
The U.S. Department of Defense has unexpectedly suspended its planned rollout of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Phase II, which was scheduled to begin on November 10, 2026. A 60-day study has been initiated to re-evaluate the program's future. Despite the suspension, defense contractors must continue to adhere to existing cybersecurity obligations, including DFARS clause 252.204-7012 and CMMC Phase I self-assessment requirements, creating uncertainty for companies that have been preparing for the more stringent third-party assessments.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: The U.S. Department of Defense has suspended its Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Phase II, pending a 60-day review of the program's future.

Article Slug: dod-suspends-cmmc-phase-ii-requirements-pending-60-day-review

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: HollowGraph Malware Uses Microsoft 365 Calendar for Covert C2
item.description:
Headline: HollowGraph Malware Uses Microsoft 365 Calendar for Covert C2

Summary:
A sophisticated espionage implant named HollowGraph is using Microsoft 365 calendar events for command-and-control (C2) and data exfiltration. The malware, discovered by Group-IB, hijacks a compromised user's calendar and hides operator instructions and stolen data within events dated to the year 2050. This novel technique allows the malware to evade detection by blending its malicious communications with legitimate Microsoft Graph API traffic, posing a significant threat of long-term, undetected persistence.

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Cyberattack, Cloud Security

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Security researchers discovered HollowGraph, a novel espionage malware that uses Microsoft 365 calendar events and the Graph API for covert C2 and data exfiltration.

Article Slug: hollowgraph-malware-abuses-microsoft-365-calendar-for-covert-c2

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ransomware Attacks on Governments Increased 13% in H1 2026
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Attacks on Governments Increased 13% in H1 2026

Summary:
A Comparitech report reveals a 13% increase in ransomware attacks targeting government entities worldwide in the first half of 2026, with 187 recorded incidents. The Gentlemen ransomware group emerged as the most active threat actor in this sector, responsible for 22 attacks. The United States was the most targeted nation, accounting for 58 incidents. Despite the rise in attack volume, the median ransom demand fell sharply from $500,000 to $100,000, suggesting a possible shift in attacker strategy towards higher volume, lower value targets.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Government entities saw a 13% increase in ransomware attacks in the first half of 2026, with The Gentlemen emerging as the most prolific threat actor in the sector.

Article Slug: government-ransomware-attacks-jump-13-percent-in-first-half-of-2026

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Miasma and Hades Worms Continue Supply Chain Attacks on npm and PyPI
item.description:
Headline: Miasma and Hades Worms Continue Supply Chain Attacks on npm and PyPI

Summary:
Self-propagating malware worms dubbed Miasma and Hades are continuing their assault on the npm and PyPI open-source ecosystems. Attributed to the financially motivated group TeamPCP, the worms spread by compromising developer accounts and publishing malicious new packages. The Hades variant, targeting PyPI, uses a novel technique involving `*-setup.pth` files to execute its payload, which harvests developer credentials and exfiltrates them to GitHub. The campaign has compromised over 100 packages, highlighting the significant and ongoing threat to the software supply chain.

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: The Miasma and Hades worms, attributed to TeamPCP, are spreading through npm and PyPI to harvest developer credentials, highlighting ongoing software supply chain risks.

Article Slug: miasma-and-hades-worms-continue-assault-on-npm-pypi-repositories

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: CISA Adds Four Actively Exploited Flaws in DD-WRT, Langflow, WordPress
item.description:
Headline: CISA Adds Four Actively Exploited Flaws in DD-WRT, Langflow, WordPress

Summary:
On July 21, 2026, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, indicating they are under active attack. The flaws include a buffer overflow in DD-WRT (CVE-2021-27137), an untrusted control sphere issue in Langflow (CVE-2026-0770), and two bugs in WordPress Core: an interpretation conflict (CVE-2026-63030) and a SQL injection (CVE-2026-60137). Federal agencies are mandated to remediate these vulnerabilities to mitigate significant risk.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Security Operations

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: CISA has added four actively exploited vulnerabilities affecting DD-WRT, Langflow, and WordPress Core to its KEV catalog, requiring urgent patching by federal agencies.

Article Slug: cisa-adds-four-actively-exploited-vulnerabilities-in-dd-wrt-langflow-wordpress-to-kev-catalog

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Manufacturing Sector Faces Precise, Targeted Attacks, SonicWall Reports
item.description:
Headline: Manufacturing Sector Faces Precise, Targeted Attacks, SonicWall Reports

Summary:
A SonicWall report finds that while overall cyberattack volume on the manufacturing sector has dropped, attackers are now using more precise, targeted strikes against the convergence of IT and OT networks. The sector recorded the highest rate of SCADA attacks and saw significant exploit activity against older vulnerabilities, including a 2021 Hikvision IP camera flaw (CVE-2021-36260) and Log4j2. This shift indicates that adversaries are moving past broad-spectrum attacks to focus on high-impact targets within the production environment.

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Cyberattack, Vulnerability

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: SonicWall's 2026 report finds the manufacturing sector is being hit by precise cyberattacks targeting OT/IT convergence, with high rates of SCADA attacks.

Article Slug: manufacturing-sector-hit-by-precise-targeted-cyberattacks-sonicwall-reports

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Data-Wiping Attacks on the Rise, Abusing Legitimate Admin Tools
item.description:
Headline: Data-Wiping Attacks on the Rise, Abusing Legitimate Admin Tools

Summary:
The supply chain industry is being warned about a rising trend of destructive data-wiping attacks. A key example is the March 2026 incident at medical tech firm Stryker, where the Iranian hacktivist group Handala Hack Team claimed to have used Microsoft Intune to issue remote wipe commands to 80,000 devices. This 'living off the land' technique, which abuses legitimate administrative tools for destructive purposes, marks a dangerous shift from financially motivated ransomware to pure, unrecoverable data destruction.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Cloud Security

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: A rising trend of data-wiping attacks is highlighted by an incident at Stryker, where threat actors used Microsoft Intune to destroy data on 80,000 devices.

Article Slug: data-wiping-attacks-rise-using-legitimate-tools-for-destruction

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: AI Compresses Attack Timelines from Weeks to Days, Sophos Reports
item.description:
Headline: AI Compresses Attack Timelines from Weeks to Days, Sophos Reports

Summary:
A Sophos report released on July 22 finds that attackers are now operationalizing artificial intelligence (AI) to dramatically speed up their attack workflows, compressing timelines from weeks to just days. The report states that AI's primary impact is not the creation of entirely new attack types, but a significant acceleration of existing methods. This trend is fueling a shift toward identity-based initial access, with attackers increasingly targeting enterprise AI identities, OAuth tokens, and APIs.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Phishing, Other

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: A new Sophos report finds that AI is dramatically accelerating cyberattack timelines and fueling a shift toward identity-based initial access and social engineering.

Article Slug: ai-compresses-cyberattack-timelines-from-weeks-to-days-sophos-reports

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Alkegen Data Breach Exposes Social Security Numbers, Health Records
item.description:
Headline: Alkegen Data Breach Exposes Social Security Numbers, Health Records

Summary:
Specialty materials manufacturer Alkegen (formerly ASP Unifrax Holdings) has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed the Social Security numbers and personal health records of affected individuals. The incident, first claimed by the Akira ransomware group on April 23, 2026, allegedly involved the theft of 57 gigabytes of corporate and employee data. Alkegen began sending notification letters to victims in July, and the incident is now under investigation by attorneys for a potential class action lawsuit.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Ransomware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Specialty materials manufacturer Alkegen has disclosed a data breach by the Akira ransomware group, which exposed Social Security numbers and health records.

Article Slug: alkegen-data-breach-exposes-social-security-numbers-and-health-records

Published Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: "Miasma" Worm Spreads Through npm Using Novel "Phantom Gyp" Evasion Technique
item.description:
Headline: "Miasma" Worm Spreads Through npm Using Novel "Phantom Gyp" Evasion Technique

Summary:
A sophisticated, self-propagating worm named 'Miasma' is actively spreading through the npm open-source registry, using a novel evasion technique dubbed 'Phantom Gyp.' The attack bypasses typical security checks by abusing 'binding.gyp' files to execute malicious code during the 'npm install' process, rather than using more commonly monitored install scripts. The worm is a credential harvester that targets a wide range of secrets from developer and CI/CD environments, including npm, GitHub, AWS, and GCP, and then uses those credentials to propagate itself further.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: A sophisticated, self-propagating worm named "Miasma" is spreading through the npm registry using a novel technique called "Phantom Gyp" to steal credentials.

Article Slug: miasma-worm-evolves-with-phantom-gyp-technique-npm-supply-chain-attack

Published Date: Thu, 02 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 02 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 12:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Sygnia: Lone Actor Used AI as 'Force Multiplier' to Accelerate Cloud Attack
item.description:
Headline: Sygnia: Lone Actor Used AI as 'Force Multiplier' to Accelerate Cloud Attack

Summary:
Cyber readiness firm Sygnia is investigating a sophisticated, financially motivated attack where a single threat actor used AI as a 'force multiplier' to compromise a global enterprise's cloud environment at unprecedented speed. The AI-assisted attack executed hundreds of unique SQL queries in parallel and used multiple access keys simultaneously, compressing activities that would normally take days into mere minutes. This incident demonstrates how AI can dramatically amplify an attacker's capabilities, allowing a lone individual to achieve the scale and speed of a large team, and highlights emerging challenges for security defenders.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence, Cloud Security

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: A Sygnia investigation reveals a lone threat actor used AI to accelerate a cloud attack on a global enterprise, executing actions at machine speed to extort the victim.

Article Slug: lone-actor-uses-ai-to-accelerate-cloud-attack-sygnia-reports

Published Date: Thu, 09 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 09 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Government agencies hit by ransomware daily in H1 2026, study finds
item.description:
Headline: Government agencies hit by ransomware daily in H1 2026, study finds

Summary:
A new study from Comparitech reveals a troubling trend: government organizations were successfully attacked by ransomware at an average rate of one per day in the first half of 2026. The 187 documented attacks mark a 13% increase from the latter half of 2025. The United States was the most frequent target, accounting for 31% of incidents. The report highlights the significant disruption to public services and data breaches resulting from these attacks, with groups like The Gentlemen, Qilin, and LockBit being the most active.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Data Breach

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: A Comparitech report reveals government agencies were hit by ransomware daily in the first half of 2026, with 187 attacks marking a 13% increase.

Article Slug: government-agencies-hit-by-ransomware-daily-in-2026-study-finds

Published Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 2

Update History: 2 updates recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Unofficial Patches Issued for 'LegacyHive' Windows Zero-Day Flaw
item.description:
Headline: Unofficial Patches Issued for 'LegacyHive' Windows Zero-Day Flaw

Summary:
A security researcher has publicly disclosed a new zero-day local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Windows User Profile Service, dubbed "LegacyHive." The flaw affects all modern versions of Windows and allows a standard user to gain administrative privileges. While Microsoft investigates the issue, security firm 0Patch has released free, unofficial micropatches to provide immediate protection against potential exploitation. The vulnerability enables an attacker to modify another user's registry hive, leading to arbitrary code execution upon the next administrator login. This proactive patching by a third party highlights the critical window of exposure between public disclosure and official vendor remediation.

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Security Operations

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: A new zero-day privilege escalation vulnerability called LegacyHive affects all modern Windows versions. While Microsoft investigates, 0Patch has issued free micropatches.

Article Slug: unofficial-patches-released-for-legacyhive-windows-zero-day-flaw

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Government Ransomware Attacks Rise 13%; 'The Gentlemen' Group Leads
item.description:
Headline: Government Ransomware Attacks Rise 13%; 'The Gentlemen' Group Leads

Summary:
Ransomware attacks targeting government entities worldwide increased by 13% in the first half of 2026, with 187 incidents recorded. A report from Comparitech identifies the United States as the most targeted nation, though attacks there have decreased. A significant shift in the threat landscape sees the emergence of 'The Gentlemen' ransomware group as the most prolific actor in this sector, responsible for 22 attacks, surpassing established groups like Qilin and LockBit. The report underscores the persistent and growing threat ransomware poses to public sector organizations globally, with incidents leading to significant data breaches and varied financial outcomes, from paid ransoms to successful system restorations.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: A new report shows ransomware attacks on government bodies rose 13% in H1 2026. The Gentlemen group is now the most active, with the U.S. being the top target.

Article Slug: ransomware-attacks-on-governments-rise-13-percent-in-h1-2026

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: New 'ENCFORGE' Ransomware Targets AI Development Environments
item.description:
Headline: New 'ENCFORGE' Ransomware Targets AI Development Environments

Summary:
A new Go-based ransomware strain named ENCFORGE has been identified by researchers at Sysdig. It specifically targets AI and machine learning infrastructure, encrypting files like model weights and datasets. The campaign is attributed to JADEPUFFER, a threat actor known for using AI agents to automate attacks. The initial access vector was a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2025-3248) in the Langflow framework. This attack signifies a tactical evolution for the actor and a new threat targeting the high-value assets of the AI development lifecycle. CISA has previously flagged other Langflow vulnerabilities in its KEV catalog, marking it as a high-risk target.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Vulnerability

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Researchers have discovered ENCFORGE, a new ransomware strain that specifically targets AI and machine learning files. The attack is linked to the JADEPUFFER group.

Article Slug: new-encforge-ransomware-targets-ai-development-environments

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ransomware Landscape Fragments as 61 New Groups Emerge in H1 2026
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Landscape Fragments as 61 New Groups Emerge in H1 2026

Summary:
The ransomware ecosystem has become increasingly fragmented, with 61 new threat groups emerging in the first half of 2026, according to a new report from Black Kite. This equates to more than one new group appearing each week. Despite this proliferation, the top five most active groups—led by Qilin, Akira, and INC Ransom—were responsible for 44% of all publicly claimed victims. The report also notes a significant decrease in the average lifespan of these groups, which has fallen to just 4.9 months, indicating a highly volatile and dynamic criminal market. Overall ransomware attacks are up 20% year-over-year, with the manufacturing and professional services sectors being the most heavily targeted.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Threat Actor

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: The ransomware ecosystem is fragmenting with 61 new groups emerging in H1 2026. A Black Kite report details the trend, noting shorter group lifespans and a 20% rise in attacks.

Article Slug: ransomware-ecosystem-fragments-as-61-new-groups-emerge-in-2026

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Supply Chain Attacks Hit 26% of UK Businesses in the Last Year
item.description:
Headline: Supply Chain Attacks Hit 26% of UK Businesses in the Last Year

Summary:
A survey by Databarracks reveals that one in four (26%) UK businesses have suffered a cyber incident originating from their supply chain in the past 12 months. The 'Data Health Check 2026' report highlights a concerning trend: 48% of IT decision-makers admit to knowingly working with suppliers that have security or resilience issues. This is often driven by a strong dependence on these suppliers, which 26% of respondents cited as a major barrier to improving their own organization's security posture. The findings underscore that while awareness of supply chain risk is high, practical and business constraints often prevent organizations from effectively mitigating it, even as cyberattacks remain the top cause of IT downtime.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: A new survey reveals 26% of UK businesses suffered a supply chain cyberattack in the past year, with nearly half knowingly working with insecure suppliers.

Article Slug: one-in-four-uk-businesses-hit-by-supply-chain-cyberattacks

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Cisco Releases Open-Weight 'Antares' AI Models for Code Analysis
item.description:
Headline: Cisco Releases Open-Weight 'Antares' AI Models for Code Analysis

Summary:
Cisco has released Antares, a family of open-weight small language models (SLMs) specifically trained to perform static application security testing (SAST) and identify security vulnerabilities in source code. The models, including Antares-1.3B and Antares-7B, are designed to run locally on standard hardware, making AI-powered security analysis more accessible to developers and security practitioners. Cisco emphasizes that Antares is intended to augment existing security workflows by accelerating the initial triage of potential flaws, not to replace comprehensive security tools or human review. The models are significantly faster than larger counterparts like GPT-4, aiming to democratize the use of AI in daily security operations.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Cisco has released Antares, a family of open-weight small language models (SLMs) designed to help developers find security vulnerabilities in source code locally and quickly.

Article Slug: cisco-releases-open-weight-ai-models-for-vulnerability-detection

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: SonicWall Zero-Days Exploited for Weeks Before Public Disclosure
item.description:
Headline: SonicWall Zero-Days Exploited for Weeks Before Public Disclosure

Summary:
Two critical zero-day vulnerabilities in SonicWall's SMA 1000 series appliances, CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, were actively exploited by threat actors for at least three weeks before being disclosed and patched. According to Volexity, attackers chained the flaws—an SSRF and a code injection—to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution with root privileges. The attackers focused on stealth and persistence, deploying custom malware named ROOTRUN and KNUCKLEBALL to maintain long-term access for credential theft and network monitoring. CISA has added both vulnerabilities to its KEV catalog, mandating urgent patching for federal agencies.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: Two critical SonicWall zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410) were exploited for weeks, allowing RCE. CISA added them to the KEV list.

Article Slug: sonicwall-zero-days-exploited-for-weeks-before-disclosure-cve-2026-15409-cve-2026-15410

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Healthcare Software Vendor Craneware Discloses Major Data Breach
item.description:
Headline: Healthcare Software Vendor Craneware Discloses Major Data Breach

Summary:
Craneware, a UK-based software supplier for the healthcare industry with a significant presence in the U.S., has announced it suffered a major data breach. The incident involved an unauthorized party gaining access to its systems and exfiltrating a 'significant volume' of files. The stolen data contains information related to employees, as well as a subset of its customers and partners. While Craneware is still investigating the full scope with third-party experts, the breach highlights the growing risk of supply chain attacks targeting the healthcare sector. The company serves over 2,000 healthcare organizations and thousands of clinics and pharmacies.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: UK-based healthcare software supplier Craneware has announced a major data breach involving the theft of employee, customer, and partner data, raising supply chain concerns.

Article Slug: healthcare-software-vendor-craneware-suffers-major-data-breach

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Exposed Server Reveals Attacker's AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit
item.description:
Headline: Exposed Server Reveals Attacker's AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit

Summary:
Researchers at Rapid7 discovered an unsecured server that exposed a sophisticated, AI-assisted toolkit for phishing and malware delivery. The operator, believed to be Russian-speaking, was targeting Windows users in Mexico with an infostealer. The toolkit shows extensive use of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate phishing lures, create exploit test plans, and manage campaigns, a workflow Rapid7 describes as 'LLM-assisted.' The server's admin panel, left with default credentials, logged over 77,000 requests in one week, primarily from Mexico. The findings provide a rare look into how cybercriminals are adopting AI to increase the efficiency and sophistication of their operations, treating them like modern software projects.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Researchers at Rapid7 discovered an exposed server hosting an AI-powered phishing toolkit used to target users in Mexico with infostealer malware.

Article Slug: ai-assisted-phishing-toolkit-discovered-on-exposed-server

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: China-Aligned APTs Use Public AI Tools in Espionage Campaigns
item.description:
Headline: China-Aligned APTs Use Public AI Tools in Espionage Campaigns

Summary:
State-sponsored threat actors aligned with China are integrating publicly available AI tools, such as Anthropic's Claude Code and DeepSeek, into their cyber-espionage campaigns. A report from Hunt.io details how the APT groups used these tools as core components to automate attacks, generate malicious scripts, and execute commands. The campaigns successfully compromised government systems in Thailand and Afghanistan, as well as financial organizations in Taiwan. This tactic of 'living off the trusted service' allows attackers to blend in, as the AI tools may already be approved for use within target networks, marking a significant evolution in APT tradecraft.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: China-linked APT groups are using public AI tools like Claude Code and DeepSeek to automate attacks and generate scripts in cyber-espionage campaigns against Asian targets.

Article Slug: china-aligned-apt-groups-leverage-public-ai-tools-in-attacks

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ernst & Young Discloses Data Breach via Third-Party IT Platform
item.description:
Headline: Ernst & Young Discloses Data Breach via Third-Party IT Platform

Summary:
Global professional services firm Ernst & Young (EY) has reported a data breach that originated from a compromised third-party IT support platform. According to a Check Point Research report, unauthorized actors gained access to the platform, potentially exposing sensitive client and employee data that was contained within IT support tickets. The name of the third-party provider has not been disclosed. This incident highlights the significant and persistent risk posed by third-party vendors, which are often targeted by attackers as a weak link to gain access to larger, more secure enterprises.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Ernst & Young (EY) has reported a data breach via a compromised third-party IT support platform, potentially exposing sensitive client and employee data from support tickets.

Article Slug: ernst-young-discloses-third-party-data-breach

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ransomware Attack Halts Production at Coca-Cola's Fairlife Dairy
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Attack Halts Production at Coca-Cola's Fairlife Dairy

Summary:
Fairlife, a U.S. dairy subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, has confirmed it was hit by a ransomware attack that severely disrupted its manufacturing operations. The attack, reported on July 20, 2026, compromised systems supporting the company's production lines, forcing a temporary nationwide halt to operations as the company enacted its incident response plan. This incident highlights the increasing threat ransomware poses to critical manufacturing and supply chain infrastructure, where attacks on operational technology (OT) can have immediate, real-world consequences beyond data theft.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Fairlife, a US dairy subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, has halted production nationwide following a ransomware attack that disrupted its manufacturing systems.

Article Slug: coca-cola-subsidiary-fairlife-halts-production-after-ransomware-attack

Published Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Ransomware Attacks in Europe Skyrocket by 55% as Supply Chains Become Prime Targets
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Attacks in Europe Skyrocket by 55% as Supply Chains Become Prime Targets

Summary:
A new report from cyber risk management firm Black Kite reveals a staggering 55.1% year-over-year increase in ransomware attacks across Europe in the first four months of 2026. The manufacturing sector was the most heavily targeted industry. Five countries—Germany, the UK, France, Italy, and Spain—accounted for nearly 70% of all incidents. The Qilin ransomware group was the most prolific, while the SafePay group showed a strong focus on German targets, highlighting a trend of geographically concentrated campaigns.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Supply Chain Attack

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Ransomware attacks in Europe surged by 55% in early 2026, with the manufacturing sector and supply chains being primary targets, according to a new report from Black Kite.

Article Slug: ransomware-attacks-in-europe-surge-by-55-percent-in-early-2026

Published Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 4

Update History: 4 updates recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: New 'LegacyHive' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Grants Admin Access
item.description:
Headline: New 'LegacyHive' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Grants Admin Access

Summary:
A security researcher has publicly disclosed 'LegacyHive,' a new zero-day privilege escalation exploit affecting fully patched Microsoft Windows systems. The proof-of-concept (PoC) abuses a flaw in the Windows User Profile Service to gain read access to another user's data, including application settings and Explorer history. No CVE has been assigned, and no patch is currently available, leaving systems vulnerable to information gathering and further attacks.

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Security Operations

Meta Description: A security researcher has publicly released a proof-of-concept exploit for a new Windows zero-day vulnerability dubbed 'LegacyHive' that allows for privilege escalation.

Article Slug: new-legacyhive-windows-zero-day-exploit-disclosed

Published Date: Sat, 18 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 18 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Inc Ransomware Exploits Critical SonicWall SMA Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Headline: Inc Ransomware Exploits Critical SonicWall SMA Zero-Day Vulnerabilities

Summary:
Two critical zero-day vulnerabilities in SonicWall's Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances are being actively exploited by the Inc Ransomware group and another threat actor, UTA0533. The chained flaws (CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410) allow unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution with root privileges. CISA has added the vulnerabilities to its KEV catalog, mandating immediate patching for federal agencies.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: Two critical zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410) in SonicWall SMA 1000 series are actively exploited by Inc Ransomware and UTA0533 for RCE.

Article Slug: inc-ransomware-exploits-sonicwall-sma-zero-day-vulnerabilities

Published Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: AI-Powered Ransomware 'JadePuffer' Deploys 'ENCFORGE' to Destroy ML Models
item.description:
Headline: AI-Powered Ransomware 'JadePuffer' Deploys 'ENCFORGE' to Destroy ML Models

Summary:
The autonomous AI threat actor JadePuffer has resurfaced, deploying a new ransomware payload named ENCFORGE. This malware is specifically designed to find and destroy AI and machine learning (ML) models, targeting over 180 file extensions common in the ML development stack. The attack, which involved a container escape, highlights a shift towards purely destructive campaigns against high-value intellectual property, with potential recovery costs for a single model reaching $500,000.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: The autonomous AI threat actor JadePuffer has resurfaced with a new ransomware, ENCFORGE, designed to find and destroy over 180 types of AI and machine learning model files.

Article Slug: ai-powered-ransomware-jadepuffer-returns-with-encforge-to-wipe-ml-models

Published Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Healthcare Software Firm Craneware Hit by Data Breach, Data Stolen
item.description:
Headline: Healthcare Software Firm Craneware Hit by Data Breach, Data Stolen

Summary:
Craneware, a UK-based software supplier for over 2,000 US hospitals, has confirmed it suffered a cyberattack resulting in a significant data breach. The company announced on July 20, 2026, that an unauthorized third party exfiltrated a 'significant volume' of data, including records belonging to employees, customers, and partners. The incident has been contained, and relevant authorities in the UK and US have been notified.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cyberattack, Supply Chain Attack

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Craneware plc, a UK-based software supplier for US hospitals, confirmed a cyberattack resulted in the exfiltration of a significant volume of customer and employee data.

Article Slug: healthcare-software-firm-craneware-suffers-data-breach

Published Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →
